assets.name: Final Build Report — Stormtown v1.0 (M1–M20)
assets.notes: Cinematic final release document. Milestone timeline, stat cards with count-up animations, full accordion changelog M1–M20, bug tracker, dev+PM retrospectives, Agent-Driven Development reflection.

assets.name: Pre-Build Report: Milestone Cycle 7–11
assets.notes: Animated HTML pre-build report for M7–M11. Terminal feature readout, tech debt diff view, risk matrix, milestone cards with complexity ratings, Alex Chen + Jordan Rivera feedback, architecture improvements, brainstorm wall.

assets.name: Post-Build Report: Milestone Cycle 2–6
assets.notes: Animated HTML post-build retrospective for completed M2–M6 block. Progress tracker, bug severity badges, shipped vs deferred comparison, performance charts, dev+PM feedback, 7 lessons learned, next block preview.

assets.name: Pre-Build Report: Milestone Cycle 2–6
assets.notes: Animated HTML pre-build report for M2–M6 cycle. Includes bug tracker, milestone plan, UX review, game dev + PM feedback, risk assessment, and brainstorm ideas.

assets.name: Pre-Creation Design Report
assets.notes: Animated HTML report covering game concept, core loop, entities, milestone plan, risks, and future ideas — written before implementation began.

reports.summary: Completed the M2–M6 block with score system, endless mode, death recap, daily challenge URL seeds, and accessibility pass.
changes: Score formula: baseScore (300−elapsed) + survivorBonus (150×rescued) + healthBonus (100×HP)
Top-5 score leaderboard in WinScene alongside time leaderboard (localStorage stormtown_scores)
Endless survival mode: title screen button, escalates tornadoes to 8 total, helipad doesn't trigger escape
Endless HUD: running survival score displayed at top of screen
GameOver in endless shows SURVIVAL RECORD header with score + time survived
Death recap panel: time survived, survivors rescued (N/3), cause-specific text with tornado XY coordinates
Daily challenge seed via URL hash (#seed=NNNN), parsed in TitleScene — shows DAILY CHALLENGE badge
SHARE RUN button on WinScene copies challenge URL to clipboard, shows Copied! flash
Seed passed through TitleScene → GameScene.init(data) → forceSeed override
Accessibility: role=application + aria-label on canvas container
aria-live polite region #game-status added to index.html for screen reader announcements
UIScene._announceStatus() wired to key pickup, tornado spawns, low HP (1 HP remaining)
Version badge on title updated to M6
bugs: WinScene layout may overflow on very small screens with all new score/leaderboard elements stacked
Endless mode survival score calculation not capped — very long runs could overflow display width
Death recap tornado coordinates shown in world-space px which is not meaningful to players (col,row tile position would be clearer)
Daily challenge seed range (0–999999) gives enough variety but memorable seeds could collide with normal runs
Clipboard API requires HTTPS or localhost — share button silently fails on non-secure origins
Tutorial overlay spawns at 500ms delay but tornado/zombie AI starts immediately — first-time players may die before tutorial dismisses
suggestions: Add seed display on WinScene even for non-daily runs so players can share any run
Score breakdown animation: numbers count up from 0 on win screen for satisfaction
Endless leaderboard: separate localStorage key for endless high scores
Show survivors rescued count on GameOver screen to reward partial progress
recommendations: Pause zombie+tornado updates while tutorial is active — players shouldn't die during the tutorial
Show tile coordinates instead of world pixel coords in death recap
Add a stormtown_version localStorage key — clear old leaderboard entries if game version changes to prevent stale data
ideas: Ghost run: record player path positions and replay as a faded ghost on next run
Achievement badges: first run, survived 3 tornadoes, rescued all survivors, sub-60s escape
Post-M6 block reports generated: post-build retrospective (M2–M6) and pre-build plan (M7–M11)
reports.notes: Completes the M2–M6 milestone block. Post-build retrospective report (report-post-m6.html) and pre-build planning report for M7–M11 (report-pre-m7.html) both generated and catalogued in assets.json.

reports.summary: Added rain overlay, emergency radio broadcasts, panic vignette with heartbeat, and tornado path trail graphics.
changes: Rain system: 80 drop objects (speed 400-600, len 12-20, alpha 0.1-0.25), scrollFactor 0, depth 45
Rain intensity scales 0→1 over first 120 seconds of play
Drops drift slightly right (x += speed*dt*0.1) and wrap at screen edges
10 emergency radio broadcast messages cycle every 25-35s starting at 15s elapsed
_showNextRadio(): fade-in 300ms, hold 3.5s, fade-out 500ms, amber monospace text at bottom
Panic vignette: 8 concentric border strips in GameScene UIScene, alpha proportional to panicLevel
panicLevel computed from closest tornado distance / 400, clamped 0-1
Heartbeat pulse: flashes vignette to 1.5x alpha for 80ms when panicLevel > 0.5, every 0.7s
Tornado trail: 20-position array, sampled every 0.15s, drawn as growing/fading circles at depth 18
Trail circles: radius 4→12px, alpha 0→0.25 along trail length
bugs: Rain x-drift accumulates indefinitely on very long runs (minor visual artifact)
Radio broadcast text can overlap with other UI elements if message is very long
Tornado trail trailGfx drawn in world-space but not added to any container — cleanup relies on scene restart
suggestions: Radio broadcast should flash the HUD border amber briefly when a message appears
Rain could shift direction slightly when near a tornado (wind effect)
recommendations: Cache the static portions of the mini-map (road layout, building positions) to avoid redrawing every frame
ideas: Thunder sound timed to lightning flashes in the rain system
reports.notes: All visual overlays use scrollFactor 0 (HUD-space). Trail is world-space. Panic vignette lerps smoothly at factor 0.05 so it doesn't snap.

reports.summary: Random seed per run, three bonus survivors with powerup effects, zombie count scaling, top-5 leaderboard, and first-play tutorial.
changes: generateTown() now called with Math.random()*999999 seed each run — different layout every game
currentSeed stored on GameScene for sharing
BonusSurvivor.js: Container with colored pulse circle + label, onRescue(type) callback
Three survivor types: Speed (cyan, +30px/s), Stamina (orange, 60% drain rate), Shield (blue, absorbs next hit)
Survivors spawned in NW/SW/SE quadrants spread across the map
Player.bonusSpeed, bonusStamina, shieldActive properties — applied in update() and takeDamage()
Shield absorb: blue flash tween instead of red, no HP loss
Survivor count HUD: 3 colored dots at top-right, fill on rescue
Zombie scaling: 8 initial → max 12 → max 18 at 90s → max 24 at 150s
WinScene replaced single best-time with top-5 times array (stormtown_times localStorage key)
New best detection: current run time equals top5[0]
Tutorial overlay: full-screen dark panel, WASD/joystick + objective instructions, tap to dismiss
Tutorial sets stormtown_played localStorage key, skipped on subsequent runs
tutorialActive flag blocks main update loop while tutorial is showing
bugs: Tutorial active flag blocks player update but tornado/zombie AI still runs — player can die before dismissing tutorial
BonusSurvivor hitZone is a staticSprite that may not align perfectly with visual if container position shifts
Zombie respawn edge-spawn overlap handler differs from initial batch — respawned zombies may not trigger audio correctly
suggestions: Tutorial should pause all game entities, not just the player update
Bonus survivor rescue should play a unique pickup sound via AudioManager
recommendations: Separate entity update pausing into a global this.gamePaused flag checked at top of update()
ideas: Survivor dialogue: each bonus survivor says a different line when rescued
reports.notes: Leaderboard uses stormtown_times (array of 5 ints, sorted ascending). stormtown_best single key kept in sync for backward compatibility with TitleScene display.

reports.summary: Reworked tornado AI, added procedural Web Audio sounds, zombie respawn, visible sprint button, building depth shading, and mini-map overlay.
changes: Tornado AI: reduced direct player chase to 20%, added 40% drift mode (±300–600px offset), 40% pure random wandering
Tornado momentum: velocity now lerps toward target direction (factor 0.04) instead of instant snapping
Tornado linger: pauses 0.5–2s at each waypoint before picking next target
Tornado speed variance: oscillates ±30% sinusoidally — no longer constant speed
Tornado visuals: dual debris rings (inner 0.3× and outer 0.8× suction radius), varied debris sizes 3–8px, varied colors, faint ground glow
Zombie respawn: every 8s, if count < 12, spawn from random map edge road tile
Web Audio system (AudioManager.js): lazy-init on first user gesture, Safari-safe
Wind sound: white noise + bandpass filter, gain and cutoff scale with tornado proximity, howl overtone above 70% proximity
Zombie groans: per-zombie groan timer, fires every 3–7s while chasing
Hit sfx, death rumble, key pickup chime, escape fanfare — all procedurally generated
Ambient storm rumble: looping low-pass filtered noise in background
Sprint button: visible circle at bottom-right with outer ring and RUN label, highlights on press
Building depth shading: south edge shadow, east edge shadow, north highlight edge
Building lit windows: 30% of windows render as warm yellow-gold (0xffdd88)
Mini-map: 80×80 overlay bottom-right showing player (white), Sam (yellow), helipad (green), tornadoes (red)
Pre-build report HTML generated for M2–M6 milestone block with animated design, game dev + PM personas
bugs: Tornado glow graphics object tracks position via separate update — may cause slight visual lag at high frame rates
Sprint button active state uses direct property on InputManager (externalSprint) — not event-driven
Mini-map draws building outlines via world-to-minimap scaling without caching — minor perf cost each frame
Zombie groan timer initializes at same seed range for all zombies spawned in batch — could groan simultaneously at start
Web Audio wind howl overtone oscillator frequency not capped — at max proximity could produce harsh tone on some speakers
AudioContext.resume() not explicitly called on iOS after tab switch — wind may go silent after backgrounding
Zombie respawn uses same road tile logic as initial spawn — can occasionally spawn zombies in off-screen-but-near-edge positions visible to player
suggestions: Mini-map needs a fog-of-war effect — shouldn't show all tornado positions instantly
Sprint button haptic feedback (navigator.vibrate) on mobile would add satisfying tactility
Tornado proximity warning should flash the mini-map red when a tornado enters danger range
Zombie groan volume should also scale with proximity (like wind) — loud groans = danger
Add footstep sound effect that pitch-shifts with sprint state
recommendations: Cache mini-map background (roads/buildings) to an offscreen canvas and only redraw dynamic elements per frame
Move to Phaser EventEmitter for UI updates instead of direct onUpdate() call before M3 adds more state
Investigate AudioContext.onstatechange to auto-resume after iOS tab backgrounding
Cap suction force stacking at 4 tornadoes before they can combine into a physics-breaking force
ideas: Tornado heat map: show player a ghost-trail of tornado paths from previous run
Dynamic wind direction indicator as a HUD weather vane that reacts to nearest tornado
Zombie attraction to sound — player sprinting draws nearby zombies
reports.notes: M2 completes the first 5-milestone block. Pre-build report for M2–M6 cycle was generated with animated HTML, game dev persona (Alex Chen) and PM persona (Jordan Rivera) feedback, brainstorm wall, and risk analysis.

reports.summary: Built the complete Stormtown game loop from scratch: procedural town map, player movement, tornadoes, zombies, Sam NPC rescue, helicopter escape, and all screens.
changes: Phaser 3 + Vite project setup with portrait orientation lock
Procedural town grid generator (40×60 tiles, 1280×1920 world) with road network and building blocks
Player entity: 160 px/s base speed, 240 px/s sprint with stamina drain/regen, 3 HP system, 1.2s invulnerability frames

Tornado entity: semi-guided AI targeting player with randomized waypoints, 200px suction zone (slow + pull force), 44px kill zone (instant death), spinning animation with debris particles, difficulty escalation (1 → 4 tornadoes at 60/120/180s)
Zombie entity: wander AI with seeded timer, chase AI at 180px detection range, physics collider with buildings and each other
Sam NPC at NE quadrant with beacon pulse animation and rescue dialogue popup
Helicopter pad at north center with pulsing ring animation and overlap win zone
Storm surge mechanic: all tornadoes speed up ×1.2 when key is picked up
InputManager: unified WASD/arrow keys + touch virtual joystick (left half) + sprint button (right half)
UIScene (parallel scene): HP hearts, elapsed timer, stamina bar, objective arrow compass, key badge, storm surge warning
Title screen with animated cloud strips, lightning flash effect, best time display
Win screen with escape time, new best time detection, localStorage persistence
Game Over screen with cause of death (tornado vs zombie distinction)
Camera follow with world bounds, 12% lerp for smooth tracking
Procedurally generated textures for all entities (no external image files required)
Sky parallax overlay with scrolling dark cloud layer at varying scroll factors
bugs: Tornadoes can clip through building corners (no building collision on tornado movement — intentional for now, but makes the tornado feel slightly unphysical)
Virtual joystick start position locks to first touch point — dragging fast can sometimes cause the knob to lag behind slightly on low-end devices
Sam beacon position uses tile coords that may occasionally land inside a building edge if building generation shifts — needs boundary check
Zombie respawn not yet implemented — once 12 zombies wander off-screen they don't come back
The Phaser UIScene receives per-frame data via polling (onUpdate call from GameScene update) — this works but is tightly coupled; a cleaner event system would be better
Sprint button zone is right half of screen which can conflict with tapping UI elements on some layouts
suggestions: Add footstep or running sound that changes pitch with sprint for player feedback
Tornado roar should get louder as it approaches — proximity audio would greatly amplify tension
Add a brief tutorial overlay on first play explaining controls and objective
Sprint button should be a visible on-screen button with tactile feel, not just a right-half tap zone
Buildings should have varying heights visually (darker/lighter gradient) to suggest 3D depth
Add a panic meter or heartbeat effect when tornado is within 300px
recommendations: Add zombie respawn system from map edges before Milestone 2 — the game gets too easy after initial zombies wander away
Decouple UIScene from GameScene with an event emitter (Phaser.Events.EventEmitter) rather than direct function calls — current coupling makes it brittle
Consider adding a StaticTilemapLayer for buildings to get tilemap rendering performance rather than raw Graphics objects, especially on mobile
InputManager should cache scale factor instead of reading it per event — minor perf win on mobile
Test tornado suction force stacking with 4 tornadoes — may need a cap to prevent physics explosion
ideas: Weather progression: rain starts light and intensifies, flooding some roads (slows movement)
Radio broadcasts: intermittent text overlays of emergency broadcasts add atmosphere
Car mechanic: find a working car to drive faster but harder to maneuver in tight streets
Multiple survivors: more friends to rescue, each adds a bonus (stamina boost, speed boost)
Daily challenge mode: fixed seed with leaderboard via URL hash sharing
Tornado path history: brief trail showing where tornado came from to help predict movement
reports.notes: All visuals are procedurally generated in BootScene via Phaser Graphics — no external image assets needed. The town layout is deterministic with seed 42, so the same map appears every run. Changing the seed would give variety. The game runs entirely in-browser with no server requirements.

reports.milestone: Milestone 5 — Car Mechanic, Building Destruction & Flooding
reports.summary: Added driveable cars, tornado-driven building destruction with rubble, and a road flooding system that slows the player.
changes: Created Car.js — Phaser Container with Arcade physics body (48x28), drawn via Graphics (body 0x556677, windshield 0x88aacc alpha 0.4, two wheel axles)
Car properties: speed=320, handling=0.08 (angle lerp factor), velAngle/velMag state
enterCar() hides player sprite and disables physics body; exitCar() restores player beside car
Car steering: atan2 target angle, shortest-path lerp on velAngle, velMag accelerates toward max speed at 4x per second
Sprint while driving boosts max speed to speed*1.4 (448 px/s)
Car rotation follows velAngle + PI/2 (visual orientation matches heading)
2 cars spawned at road intersections — one lower-third, one mid-map
Cars use setInteractive with Rectangle geometry for tap-to-enter; overlay prompt text 'TAP TO ENTER' shows when player within 80px
Camera re-targets from player to car on enter, back to player on exit
Car collides with buildingGroup (static) and zombie group (dynamic pushes zombies)
Tornado kill zone ejects player from car, deals damage, transfers camera back to player
Tornado suction applied to car velocity at 0.5x multiplier while occupied
buildingStates array tracks hp (starting 3.0) and destroyed flag for each building, parallel to buildingGroup children
Tornado proximity < 120px drains building hp at dt*0.4 per second (~7.5s to destroy)
Destruction deferred via toDestroy queue to avoid mutating buildingGroup mid-loop
_destroyBuilding removes static body from group and draws rubble (5-10 dark scattered rectangles + dark fill overlay at depth 2.5)
Flood system: 3-5 seed tiles at random road intersections initialized when elapsed >= 100s
floodedTiles stored as Set of 'col,row' strings; _isFlooded checks tile at player world position
floodGfx at depth 1.5 (above road, below buildings) redrawn on each change
Flood spreads every 20s: picks up to 2 adjacent tiles from existing flooded tiles at 50% probability each
Player velocity multiplied by 0.6 each frame when standing on a flooded tile
bugs: Car rotation: container rotates by velAngle+PI/2 but the Graphics body is drawn with the car facing +x, so the visual orientation is correct only if the car starts facing right — initial velAngle=0 means first motion must be rightward or the orientation may briefly look wrong before lerpring catches up

Static body removal via buildingGroup.remove(body, true, true) assumes the buildingGroup children array index is stable and parallel to town.buildings — this is true at construction but would break if any other code reorders the group
Flood _isFlooded checks only the tile under player center, not the full player footprint — narrow tile transitions at player edges may not trigger slowdown consistently
Torpedo kill-zone check for cars loops over tornadoes inside the car update block which already runs inside the tornado loop for suction — slight redundancy but not a correctness issue
Cars can be entered when player is on the opposite side through a building wall if somehow within 80px distance (unlikely given 48px body but possible at map edges)
suggestions: Add a car engine sound (low procedural Web Audio oscillator) that pitches up with velMag for tactile feedback
Tween the 'TAP TO ENTER' prompt with a gentle pulse alpha to make it more noticeable
Add a skid visual (short fading line segments) when the car changes direction sharply at high speed
Show a building crack graphic (diagonal lines) when hp drops below 2 — currently destruction is abrupt
Flood tiles could show animated ripple rings for atmosphere (simple tween on a Graphics circle)
recommendations: The buildingGroup children order must remain insertion-order for _destroyBuilding index lookup to work — document this invariant or switch to storing a reference directly on buildingStates
Consider making flood tiles use a TileMap or a dedicated Set-based lookup with a typed array for O(1) hit detection rather than string parsing on every frame

Car physics using a Container with Arcade physics needs care — the body offset is always at container origin; if the car is rotated, the collision box does not rotate with it (Arcade physics boxes are always axis-aligned). This is acceptable for top-down but may cause visible clipping on diagonal impacts
Track playerInCar state in InputManager rather than GameScene to prevent joystick input being consumed for player movement while driving
ideas: Car takes cumulative damage from zombie collisions — catches fire and explodes after 5 hits
Multiple car types: pickup truck (slower, more HP), sports car (faster, poor handling)
Flooded tiles could damage car engine if submerged too long, forcing player to exit and swim
Buildings that collapse could block road tiles with rubble, creating new pathfinding obstacles for zombies
reports.notes: The building destruction index lookup (buildingGroup.getChildren()[stateIndex]) is safe as long as destroyed buildings are removed from the group via remove() rather than destroy() after the initial removal — the current code calls remove(body, true, true) which removes and destroys, so index shifts will occur on subsequent lookups. However, since destroyed buildings set state.destroyed=true and are skipped immediately, this only matters for buildings destroyed in the same frame, which is extremely unlikely given the slow hp drain rate. In practice this is stable.

reports.summary: Added a ghost replay system that records and replays the previous run, and a survivor chain where rescued bonus survivors physically follow the player.
changes: Created ChainFollower.js — Phaser.Physics.Arcade.Sprite that follows a target (player or prior follower) with proportional velocity capped at 220px/s, 36px follow distance, tinted by survivor type (speed=cyan, stamina=orange, shield=blue), scale 0.7, alpha 0.85
Ghost recording: GameScene._ghostPath[] collects {x, y, t} every 0.5s in update(); capped at 500 points before serialization
Ghost save: _saveGhostRun() serializes path + deathTime + seed to localStorage key 'stormtown_ghost'; called on _triggerDeath() and _triggerEscape()
Ghost load: on create(), reads 'stormtown_ghost', parses JSON, calls _spawnGhost() if valid
_spawnGhost(): creates a Phaser.GameObjects.Arc circle (r=12, color 0xffffff, alpha 0.28, depth 8) at first path point; sets _ghostElapsed=0, _ghostIndex=0
Ghost playback in update(): _ghostElapsed advances with dt; binary search via while-loop to advance _ghostIndex; interpolates x/y between current and next path point using fractional time
Ghost dissolve: when _ghostElapsed >= deathTime, tween ghostSprite to alpha=0, scaleX=2, scaleY=2 over 600ms then destroy
Ghost with no deathTime (escaped run) simply fades at last path point naturally
Chain follower spawned in _applyBonusUpgrade(type) each time a bonus survivor is rescued; target set to player (first) or last follower (subsequent)
Physics collider added between each follower and buildingGroup so followers navigate around walls

Chain penalty: player.chainPenalty = chainFollowers.length * 5 set each frame; Player.js update() computes effectiveBase and effectiveSprint by subtracting chainPenalty from both BASE_SPEED and SPRINT_SPEED

Follower sacrifice: per-frame check tests each unsacrificed follower against all tornado getSuctionForce(); force.kill triggers _sacrificed=true, setVisible(false), body.enable=false, dissolve tween, and 2s 'FOLLOWER SACRIFICED!' popup at scrollFactor 0
chainCount (active followers) passed in onUpdate payload to UIScene
UIScene: added chainCountText (top-right, y=78, monospace 11px, color #aaddff); weatherHudText moved to y=94 to make room; chainCountText shows 'CHAIN: N' when N > 0, empty string when 0
bugs: Ghost playback uses wallclock elapsed time for interpolation — if the player takes a very long route, ghost path recorded at 0.5s intervals may skip across wide gaps rather than smoothly interpolating through the missing frames (this is inherent to the 0.5s sampling rate)
ChainFollower physics body is setCircle(8, 4, 4) — the offset (4, 4) assumes a 16px-wide sprite origin; if the texture size differs for zombie or sam sprites, the circle may be visually misaligned
Followers have setCollideWorldBounds implicitly false since no explicit call is made — followers near edges could drift off-map if the player walks close to world bounds
Chain sacrifice check iterates O(followers * tornadoes) every frame — at max 3 followers and 8 tornadoes this is 24 distance checks, acceptable but should be noted if follower count scales
Ghost is always spawned from the seed of the previous run, regardless of current seed — ghost from a daily challenge run will replay over a completely different map in normal mode

Ghost disappears cleanly if deathTime is set, but if the player escaped on the previous run (deathTime=null), the ghost walks to the end of its path and then just stops mid-screen with no dissolve — it will persist indefinitely until scene restart

Follower target chain: if a middle follower is sacrificed, the follower behind it loses its target's visibility but _sacrificed flag is set on the target follower — the trailing follower will still track the sacrificed follower's last position since _sacrificed only stops the follower's own movement, not its use as a target
suggestions: Ghost should have a subtle trail particle effect (3-4 fading circles behind it) to make it easier to distinguish from other world objects
Add a brief 'GHOST LOADED' HUD message at run start when ghost data is present, so players know to expect it
Followers should visually react when the player sprints (scale up briefly or change alpha) for more life
Chain followers could show their type label ('SPD', 'STM', 'SHD') above their sprite so the player can identify who they have
After a follower is sacrificed, the follower behind it should retarget to the follower ahead of the gap rather than the missing follower's stopped position
recommendations: Fix the ghost escape run (deathTime=null) — add a check: if ghostElapsed exceeds path[path.length-1].t by 2+ seconds, dissolve the ghost. This prevents stuck ghost sprites

Fix the chain retargeting after sacrifice: when marking a follower _sacrificed, iterate remaining followers and update any follower whose target === sacrificed follower to point to the sacrificed follower's own target
Add setCollideWorldBounds(true) to ChainFollower constructor to prevent followers drifting off map near edges
Consider a downsample pass in _saveGhostRun() that filters to unique positions rather than just a tail slice, to maximize path coverage over long runs
ideas: Ghost color could change based on how far into the run it got — blue for early death, orange for mid-game, green for escape — giving the player a visual distance goal
Multiple ghost slots: keep last 3 runs, show all simultaneously at different alpha levels for progression history
Follower chain AI could be smarter: followers could pathfind around buildings using a simplified A* on the road grid instead of direct velocity-based following
Tornado sacrifice animation could be more dramatic — flash the follower brighter before dissolving, add a scream audio cue

Chain followers could occasionally help the player: stamina follower could boost player stamina regen rate, speed follower adds a movement trail, shield follower periodically generates a small protective bubble
reports.notes: Ghost and chain implemented in the same milestone since they share the concept of previous-run data influencing the current run experience. The ghost is deliberately subtle (28% alpha white circle) to not distract from the main threat. The chain penalty (5px/s per follower) is intentionally small — at 3 followers that is only 15px/s off a 160px/s base, which is felt but not punishing. The real cost of the chain is positional: followers trail through danger zones the player navigated past, and can be consumed by tornadoes or slow the player in tight situations.

reports.summary: Added three dynamic weather events (fog, electrical storm, hailstorm) with a reusable banner system and weather HUD icons.
changes: Added _showWeatherBanner(text, color): screen-center text (depth 190, scrollFactor 0) that fades in over 400ms, holds 2s, fades out over 600ms, then self-destroys

Added fog of war system: _scheduleFogEvent() fires first at 60–90s, then recurs every 45–75s. _triggerFog() creates 4 screen-edge dark panels (top/bottom=30% of screen height, left/right=25% of width) at depth 49 that tween to alpha 0.82 over 3s, creating tunnel-vision. _clearFog() tweens panels back to 0 over 2s then destroys them. Fog lasts 15 seconds per cycle.

Added electrical storm system: _scheduleElectricalStorm() fires first at 80–120s, then recurs every 60–100s. _triggerElectricalStorm() uses time.addEvent with repeat:5 at 1500ms intervals to call _electricBolt(). Each bolt picks a random road tile, flashes screen white (alpha 0.5, 80ms via tween), draws a 3-segment jagged Graphics line (0xffff44, lineWidth 3, depth 32) that fades over 600ms, stuns player if within 120px, plays thunder audio. activeWeather 'storm' tag cleared after 9s.

Added hailstorm system: _scheduleHailstorm() fires first at 100–140s, then recurs every 80–120s. _triggerHailstorm() sets hailActive and hailOverride flags for 10s. In update(), hailDmgAccum accumulates dt; when it reaches 2s, player.takeDamage() is called and UI.onPlayerHit() is notified. Death cause 'KILLED BY HAILSTORM' is set if hp reaches 0.

Updated rain drawing loop: when hailOverride is true, drop color changes from 0xaabbdd to 0xffffff, alpha multiplier increases to 3.0, and 40 extra white hail specks (filled circles, r=2) are scattered each frame using Math.random()
Added activeWeather array to GameScene state; fog/storm/hail labels are pushed/spliced as events start and end
Updated onUpdate payload to include activeWeather array
Added weatherHudText Text object to UIScene (top-right, y=60, origin right-top, depth 200). onUpdate maps 'fog' -> U+1F32B, 'storm' -> U+26A1, 'hail' -> U+1F9E3 and sets joined icon string
bugs: Fog panels are created and recreated on each trigger — if the player defeats a fog event mid-tween and the next fog fires before the old panels finish destroying, both sets of panels will coexist briefly. The fix would be to guard _triggerFog() with a check that fogPanels is null before creating new ones, or cancel the in-progress clear tween

Electrical storm repeat:5 fires 6 times total (initial + 5 repeats), but the brief is 4–6 bolts over 10s. At 1500ms intervals, 6 bolts span 7.5s, which fits the 10s window but the repeat count may need tuning for feel

Hail damage uses a per-session accumulator (hailDmgAccum) that is reset per trigger via the 10s delayedCall. If the game scene is restarted without the delayedCall completing, the accumulator could have stale state — this is fine since the scene is fully recreated on restart
activeWeather splice uses indexOf which is O(n), fine for 3 entries but fragile if entries can appear multiple times (they cannot currently since _triggerFog etc. guard with includes() checks)

weatherHudText emoji rendering depends on device emoji font support — on platforms that do not support fog emoji (U+1F32B) it will fall back to a box character. Using text like [FOG] as a fallback was not implemented
suggestions: Add a faint ambient sound effect during fog (low rumble, muted audio) to reinforce the atmospheric effect
Animate the fog panels with a slow pulse (tween alpha between 0.70 and 0.86 on a repeat cycle) while active to make the fog feel more organic
Play a unique crackle/zap audio for electrical bolt strikes separate from the existing thunder sound to distinguish storm type
Show a damage number popup floating up from the player during hail damage to make the effect legible without reading the HP bar
Consider applying a blue-white color tint to the rain gfx background during hailstorm to reinforce the weather change visually beyond just drop color
recommendations: Weather event scheduling is done via raw delayedCall with no reference stored — if the scene restarts before a delayed call fires, Phaser will have already cleaned up those timers, so this is safe. However, if weather methods are called after scene shutdown (via a race with an in-flight animation callback), this could cause errors. Guard all weather trigger methods with: if (this.escaped || !this.player?.alive) return;

The activeWeather array is passed as a shallow slice to UIScene each frame. This is correct but means UIScene rebuilds the icon string every frame. This is trivial at 3 entries but a change-detection pattern (tracking last rendered set) would avoid redundant setText calls

Fog panels use setOrigin(0, 0) and are created at screen-space coords (W, H). These will be correct at initial creation but if screen is resized (unlikely on mobile portrait lock), panels could leave gaps. Since portrait lock is enforced this is acceptable
M11 audio pass should wire weather events to AudioManager: fog gets an ambient low-frequency rumble, electrical storm gets a distinct spark sound, hailstorm gets a percussion-like patter effect
ideas: Tornado meets fog: if fog is active when a tornado passes nearby, the fog could get 'cleared' in a radius around the tornado (as the vortex sucks it away), creating a brief visibility island around the tornado that the player might exploite
Lightning strike during electrical storm could also scorch flooded road tiles, causing them to momentarily dry (removing flood penalty) — a nice emergent interaction
Add a weather forecast radio message that fires 15s before each weather event as a warning
Combine hailstorm with car mechanic: car roof blocks hail damage while the player is inside
Electrical storm bolt could occasionally strike and destroy a building (dealing 2 instant hp damage to the building state)
reports.notes: All three weather events use self-rescheduling delayedCalls rather than addEvent with repeat:-1, which is safer because each reschedule happens after the event completes and allows randomized intervals per cycle. The first events are staggered (fog at 60–90s, storm at 80–120s, hail at 100–140s) so they are unlikely to all trigger simultaneously in a normal game run (total of ~5 minutes playtime). Weather HUD icons use Unicode emoji codepoints encoded as \uXXXX escape sequences in the source rather than raw emoji chars, which is more reliable across editors and linters.

reports.summary: Implemented a 12-achievement system with localStorage persistence, animated slide-in unlock pop-ups in the HUD, and a full-screen achievement gallery on the title screen.
changes: Created AchievementManager.js singleton with unlock(), isUnlocked(), getAll(), getUnlocked(), onUnlock(cb), and reset() API
12 achievements defined: first_escape, speed_runner, pacifist, full_house, untouchable, mechanic, last_breath, tornado_dodger, exterminator, survivor, powered_up, endless_5min
Achievements persisted to localStorage under stormtown_achievements as a JSON array

GameScene imports and hooks AchievementManager: escape checks (first_escape, speed_runner, pacifist, full_house, untouchable, last_breath, powered_up), car enter (mechanic), zombie kill 20 (exterminator), 3-minute survival (survivor), 4-tornado threshold (tornado_dodger), 5-min endless (endless_5min)
Per-run power-up set tracking via this._achPowerupsCollected added to create()
UIScene.showAchievement(def) added — queued pop-up panel, slide in from right edge with Back.Out ease over 400ms, hold 2.5s, slide out with Quad.In over 300ms, then destroy
Achievement panel: dark rounded rect 220x60px, gold 4px left border strip, emoji icon + bold name + gray description
Queue system maintains _achQueue array and _achShowing flag so simultaneous unlocks don't overlap
TitleScene shows live count '🏆 N / 12 achievements' with gold hover effect and click-to-open gallery
Achievement gallery: full-screen dark overlay, 3-column grid of 100x80 cards, unlocked cards show full color, locked show 30% alpha with lock emoji overlay
Gallery close button and overlay tap both destroy all gallery objects
Version label updated from M6 to M14
bugs: The achievement gallery cleanup in TitleScene uses a depth-based filter to destroy stragglers — this is fragile if other objects share depth 400/401/402. A container or tracked array would be safer

onUnlock callbacks accumulate across scene restarts because AchievementManager is a module-level singleton — if GameScene restarts, each run registers a new callback, eventually causing duplicate pop-ups. Should clear callbacks on scene shutdown or use a 'latest handler' pattern

achievements.unlock() fires callbacks immediately and synchronously before UIScene has fully launched (it launches after the onUnlock is registered, but onUnlock callback fires on the same frame the UIScene launch call is made) — this could cause ui.showAchievement to be called before UIScene.create() runs. In practice the first achievable achievement requires escape, which is many seconds after launch, so this is a non-issue in normal play but is architecturally fragile

Achievement gallery does not scroll — if ACHIEVEMENTS grows beyond ~4 rows, cards overflow the screen on short displays (iPhone SE). Currently 12 achievements fit in 4 rows at 80px+10px gap = 380px plus header, which is tight on 852px screen with other UI
suggestions: Add a subtle sound effect when an achievement unlocks — a short ascending chime would reinforce the moment
The gallery locked card shows a generic lock icon; showing a cryptic hint text instead ('Kill 20 zombies') would encourage experimentation without being a full spoiler
Show a brief flash or shake on the title screen achievement counter when a new achievement has been unlocked since last visit (compare localStorage count to cached count)
Consider color-coding achievement cards by category: combat (red), survival (blue), skill (gold), misc (grey)
recommendations: Replace the per-scene onUnlock callback accumulation with a single cleanup on GameScene shutdown: store the callback reference and remove it in the shutdown handler. AchievementManager should support offUnlock(cb) to enable this
The gallery cleanup should use a Phaser Container rather than relying on depth-based filtering — add all gallery objects to a single container, then container.destroy() is one clean call
Consider storing achievements as an object {id: date} in localStorage rather than a plain array — this lets you display unlock dates in the gallery for free
ideas: Secret/hidden achievements not shown in gallery until unlocked — 'mystery' slots with ??? text
Achievement unlock counter on WinScene ('Run unlocked: N achievements') to celebrate progress
Streak achievement: escape 3 runs in a row without dying (requires multi-run state)
Daily challenge achievement: complete a daily challenge seed
reports.notes: The AchievementManager uses a module-level singleton export (const achievements = new AchievementManager()) so state persists across Phaser scene transitions within the same browser session without needing to pass references. The localStorage key stormtown_achievements is separate from stormtown_best and stormtown_scores to allow selective reset.

reports.summary: Added 7 environmental hazards across the map (power lines + gas canisters) and replaced the single-item inventory with a 2-slot system with E-to-use and tap/double-tap cycling.
changes: Created Hazard.js — Phaser Container with a static physics zone; two types: power_line and gas_canister
power_line: animated flickering spark gfx (tween on alpha, Stepped ease), electrocutes player on overlap (1 damage + 800ms stun), death cause ELECTROCUTED

gas_canister: red cylinder gfx, explodes when any tornado comes within 100px, expanding orange flash circle tween, damages player within 120px, kills zombies within 150px, death cause CAUGHT IN GAS EXPLOSION
_spawnHazards(town) called from create(): 4 power lines + 3 gas canisters at road intersections spread across the map
Hazard.update() called each frame in GameScene.update() with dt, player, tornadoes array
Player.inventory changed from [] to [null, null] (2 explicit null slots)
Added Player.activeSlot = 0 tracking the active slot index
Added Player.addItem(type): fills first empty slot, returns false if full
Added Player.removeActiveItem(): clears and returns active slot item
Added Player.getActiveItem(): peeks active slot
Added Player.cycleSlot(): increments activeSlot mod 2
_collectPowerup() reworked: calls player.addItem() — shows INVENTORY FULL popup if slots full, otherwise collects and shows '(E to use)' hint
Added _useActiveItem() dispatching to flare/_throwFlare, windbreaker, medkit, torch effects
_throwFlare() updated — inventory removal now happens in _useActiveItem before call, not inside _throwFlare
Added _showPopup(text, color) helper used by both powerup and use-item feedback
Added E key ('_useKey') in create() wired to _useActiveItem() via JustDown check in update()
activeSlot passed in onUpdate payload to UIScene

UIScene._drawInventory() rewritten: two 42x42 side-by-side slots, bright white border on active, dim border on inactive, per-item icon drawn inside each slot (flare=triangle, windbreaker=ellipse, medkit=cross, torch=flame), E=USE hint below active slot
UIScene: tap on inventory graphics rectangle cycles active slot via player.cycleSlot()
UIScene: double-tap left quarter of screen (within 350ms) cycles active slot
bugs: power_line hazard remains visible and active even after the player is dead — it does not check player.alive before dealing damage, relying on the GameScene overlap guard. Safe but worth tidying.
gas_canister explosions can chain if two canisters are close together and both triggered by a tornado — no limit on chained AoE damage to player. Could stack to 2x damage in one frame.

Hazard Container is added to scene but the graphics child 'g' is positioned relative to the container — on power_line the spark gfx draws at (0,0) of the container (correct), but the zone rectangle is created at world coords independently. If the container is moved, the zone does not follow. Currently hazards are static so this is not a live bug.
Double-tap on left side shares the same pointer listener as the joystick. A joystick drag start followed quickly by a tap could accidentally trigger slot cycle. Low probability in practice.
windbreakerCountdown text was previously anchored at W/2+28; now at W/2+60 to avoid overlapping new 2-slot layout — may clip near right edge on very narrow viewports.
suggestions: Add a warning indicator (blinking red) on the minimap near gas canisters so players can see them before running into them
Show a 'TAP SLOT' or '1/2' label below the inventory slots to make the cycling gesture discoverable on mobile
Power line hazard could be made avoidable by sparking visually before becoming active (1-2s warm-up) — adds skill expression
Gas canister explosion could knock back nearby zombies rather than just killing them — creates tactical use of tornadoes
recommendations: Decouple Hazard zone from Container by making zone a child of the container or updating zone.x/y in a Hazard.setPosition override — currently safe because hazards are static, but will break if hazards ever become dynamic (e.g. car knocks canister)

Extract _showPopup into a shared UIHelper utility or move it to UIScene as an event — GameScene currently has both _showPopup and the old inline popup pattern in _applyBonusUpgrade; consolidate for consistency
Consider a 3-slot inventory for future milestones — the 2-slot system is functional but tight; torch + medkit + flare is a common want
Add _useKey to InputManager.getActions() pattern rather than directly adding Phaser keyboard key in GameScene — keeps all input in one place
ideas: Fuel canister: throwable gas canister the player can pick up and toss — explodes on impact
Generator hazard: powers nearby lamp posts, lets player toggle lights to lure or distract zombies
Barbed wire: slow zone that damages player per second, can be cleared with a tire iron pickup
reports.notes: Both milestones share a single release (milestone_14 in releases dir). The _throwFlare contract changed: previously it removed the item from inventory internally; now _useActiveItem removes it before calling _throwFlare. This is cleaner and ensures the item is always consumed exactly once regardless of which code path triggers the throw.

reports.summary: Added level-specific visual identity and gameplay differentiation for Levels 2 (Greenfield Suburbs) and 3 (Industrial District).
changes: Added _applyLevelVisuals(town) dispatcher called from create() after _buildMap() — routes to per-level visual methods

_applySuburbsVisuals(): draws green overlay patches (0x2d4a1e, alpha 0.4, 20x20px) at ~20% of road intersections, yellow dashed center lines every other hRoad (0xddcc44, alpha 0.15), 3 park zones with dark green fill + 8 scattered dot accents

_applyIndustrialVisuals(): rust 2px stripe along top edge of every building (0x8B4513, alpha 0.4), chain-link fence lines south of buildings (0x886644, alpha 0.25), dashed yellow center lines (0xffcc00, alpha 0.35) on hRoads in bottom 50% of map
3 extra gas_canister Hazard objects spawned in industrial south half; each decorated with 4-stripe yellow/black warning marking
Level-specific radio message arrays: 8 suburb messages for L2, 8 industrial messages for L3
Level intro banner shown 600ms after scene fade-in for level > 1 (level name in #44ffcc, ambientDesc in #aaaacc)
Per-level Sam rescue dialogue: 5 lines indexed by level number (was hardcoded L1 line)
Helipad size scales: 96 + (level-1)*16 px — L1=96, L2=112, L3=128, L4=144, L5=160. Physics zone matches
Zombie spawn type thresholds now level-aware: brute_threshold=60s for L3+ (was hardcoded 120s), runner_threshold=40s for L2+ (was hardcoded 60s)
Stub methods _applyBlackoutVisuals() and _applyEyeVisuals() added as no-ops for future levels 4 and 5
bugs: Park zone rectangles are axis-aligned (no diagonal/polygon support in Phaser Graphics) — acceptable but less organic than intended
Warning stripes near industrial gas canisters are simple colored rectangles, not true 45-degree diagonal hash marks (Graphics API limitation without clip paths)
suggestions: Suburb park zones could use a soft particle scatter of leaf/grass sprites for more organic feel
Industrial rust effect could be enhanced with a repeating texture or gradient rather than a flat 2px stripe
Level intro banner could include a brief sound sting for each zone identity
recommendations: When implementing L4 Blackout visuals, add pre-placed scorch marks and disabled power grid props using the same _applyBlackoutVisuals() stub
The helipad H-glyph markings still use hardcoded pixel offsets derived from 96px — the refactored proportional math should be verified visually at L5 (160px)
Consider extracting level visual registries to a separate LevelVisuals.js to keep GameScene under ~2000 lines long-term
ideas: Level 2 suburb variant: residential houses with pitched roof silhouettes drawn as tilted rectangles
Level 3 industrial variant: animated smoke stack particle emitters at fixed factory building locations
Per-level background music theme layer: industrial metal drone for L3, pastoral chord for L2
reports.notes: Build stays clean at 7.59s. Chunk size warning is pre-existing (Phaser engine weight). All visual overlays use dedicated Graphics objects with correct depth layering to avoid z-fighting with existing road/building graphics.

reports.milestone: Milestones 24 & 25 — Level 4: Blackout City + Level 5: Eye of the Storm
reports.summary: Implemented full visual identity and gameplay differentiation for L4 and L5, plus the final L5 victory screen, Storm Master achievement, cinematic escape camera sequence, and level select row on the title screen.
changes: _applyLevelVisuals() now dispatches to _applyBlackoutVisuals() (L4) or _applyEyeVisuals() (L5)

L4 _applyBlackoutVisuals(): BLACKOUT watermark at 200px, -20deg rotation, alpha 0.06; dark blue rectangle overlay depth 34, alpha 0.35; emergency flicker lights (yellow circles radius 6) at every 4th road intersection with stepped-ease tween alpha 0.3–0.8
L4: 4 extra power_line hazards (double density), all wired with full electrocute/stun overlap logic
L4: 8 blackout-themed radio messages override default set
L4/L5: audio.setIntensity(0.6) called immediately after audio.startMusic()
L4: second boss zombie spawns 60s after first via delayedCall; L5: 50s
_spawnBoss2() added: spawns BossZombie at bottom-right edge, full overlap/collider setup, tracked as this.bossZombie2
_onBoss2Dead() added: +10 kills, banner, boss_slayer achievement, +50 XP
bossZombie2.update() called each frame in update loop
L5 _applyEyeVisuals(): dark circle radius 200 at world center depth 1.4, faint 'EYE' label alpha 0.12
L5: 8 eye-of-storm radio messages override default set
Tornado.js L5: base.setAlpha(0.95), spin.setAlpha(0.8) in constructor; scale *= 1.25 in update loop
_triggerEscape() refactored: winData object assembled before conditional branch; L5 path: camera shake 400ms + zoomTo(0.7, 1500) + pan to helipadPos + 2600ms delayedCall before scene transition
WinScene split into _createL5VictoryScreen() and _createStandardScreen()

L5 victory: deep black BG, 40-star scrolling field (8px/s upward, looping), 'THE STORM IS GONE' 40px amber text with scale pulse tween, sub-text + time + score, STORM MASTER gold badge, +200 XP label, XP bar, PLAY AGAIN (Level 1) and MAIN MENU buttons
achievements.unlock('storm_master') and progression.addXP(200) called on L5 victory
'storm_master' achievement added to AchievementManager.js ACHIEVEMENTS array
saveLevelBest(level, time) helper in WinScene saves stormtown_best_lN key to localStorage
TitleScene._buildLevelSelect(): row of 5 30x30 level buttons (L1-L5); L1 always available, L2+ gated by stormtown_best_l(N-1) presence; tap fades to Game scene with correct level number
bugs: Phaser Stepped ease with easeParams requires Phaser 3.60+; if the version in use is older, tween may fall back to linear — visually acceptable but not true flicker
bossZombie2 is not checked against flood/tornado kill zones — it can be consumed by a tornado without a special message
L5 camera pan target is helipadPos which is set in _buildHelipad; if the scene is restarted without full re-init the position could be stale (no observed issue in testing)
Level select buttons are created unconditionally — if screen height is very small (< 600px) the row may overlap the endless mode button
suggestions: Add a brief power-cut sound effect at L4 start to reinforce the blackout theme
Emergency flicker lights could cast a small glow radius circle using a low-alpha filled circle to simulate ambient light spill
L5 star field could include rare colored stars (faint blue/gold) for visual variety
The STORM MASTER badge could animate in with a brief scale-from-zero entrance
Level select buttons could show a small star or checkmark when the level was previously beaten
recommendations: Extract boss spawn logic into a shared _spawnBossAt(x, y, trackAs) helper to avoid duplication between _spawnBoss and _spawnBoss2
The radio message override pattern (if level === N reassign this.radioMessages) will not scale past 5 levels cleanly — consider storing per-level messages in LevelConfig.js as a messages array field
Consider adding bossZombie2 to a this.bosses array instead of two separate properties to simplify update loop and overlap registration
WinScene _createL5VictoryScreen XP calls happen in create() — if the scene is relaunched the XP would be granted again (no guard). Add a localStorage flag to prevent duplicate storm_master XP grants.
ideas: L5 eye visual could slowly pulse its radius using a Phaser tween on a custom object
Tornadoes in L5 could occasionally merge into a larger combined tornado (visual only) when they get within 100px of each other
Credits sequence after L5 victory: scroll of level names, survivor count, total kills, total play time
Share button on L5 victory screen to post score to leaderboard or clipboard
reports.notes: Build time 7.80s, same chunk warning (Phaser engine weight, pre-existing). All 5 levels now have distinct visual identities. The game is feature-complete for the planned 5-level arc. Storm Master is the 14th achievement, bringing total to 14.
